card_page.policy_text: Cards & Borrowing | Services | Framingham Public Library Library Catalog Website Events Library Catalog Website Events Library Catalog Website Events Español Português Catalog My Account McAuliffe Branch Friends of the Library Literacy Unlimited FPL Foundation About Contact Us Hours & Locations Mission & History Library Policies Board of Trustees Friends of the Library Framingham Public Library Foundation Literacy Unlimited Community Partners Volunteer Work for the Library Long-Range Plan, 2027–2032 Parking Updates and Information Browse Books for Adults Audiobooks Movies & TV Music Magazines & Newspapers Seed Lending Library Library of Things Languages Zine and Small Press Collection Services Cards & Borrowing Museum Passes Job Search Assistance Technology at the Library FPL Booklocker Bookmobile Spark Lab at Framingham Public Library Information & Research Assistance Local History & Genealogy The Citizenship Corner Veterans Resource Center Social Services at the Main Library Massachusetts Access to Justice Find Online Digital Collection: eBooks and More Research Resources Consumer Reports Mango Languages Finding Your Way Yearbooks Events & Programs Events Calendar Monthly Newsletter Library Card Design Contest Lifelong Learning Programs Summer Reading Program Community Events Dark Summer Book Fest Kids & Teens Kids Teens Homework Center (Returning Monday, September 29 2025!) How do I? Get a Library Card Reserve a Book Renew a Book Online Download an eBook Ask a Question Reserve a Museum Pass Book a Meeting/Study Room Support the Library Recommend an Item for Purchase Make a Suggestion Sign up for our Newsletter Use the App Services Cards & Borrowing Services Services Cards & Borrowing Museum Passes Job Search Assistance Technology at the Library FPL Booklocker Bookmobile Spark Lab at Framingham Public Library Information & Research Assistance Local History & Genealogy Framingham History Framingham Oral Histories Framingham Genealogy The Citizenship Corner Veterans Resource Center Social Services at the Main Library Little Free Pantry at FPL Massachusetts Access to Justice Cards & Borrowing Library Cards Massachusetts residents may obtain a library card in person by presenting identification and proof of current address. There is no waiting period for a library card. You must be at least five years old to get a card. You may sign up for a temporary card online , which allows you to download eBooks and access state provided databases. Please read the Minuteman page on temporary cards for more information. Please notify the library of any change of address. Please present your library card when borrowing materials. Lost library cards should be reported immediately. You are responsible for all materials borrowed on your card until you report it lost or stolen. Parents are responsible for materials borrowed by children 18 years and younger. Library cards expire periodically so that we may verify current contact information. If your card expires, please contact us to update your card. If a card is completely inactive for a period of four years, it will be deleted from the database. Checking Your Library Account Online Using your library card number and PIN, you can log into your account to view your library record, which includes both print and digital content. To see your digital content only, click HERE . If you have a smartphone or tablet, consider downloading the Minuteman Library Network app to your device. Either way, you'll be able to see what materials you've checked out, renew items, check the status of or cancel your reserves, modify your PIN or email address, and more. Borrowing Materials Audiobooks Type Length Limit Renewals? Adult 3 weeks 10 total audiobooks 2 Adult New 2 weeks 3 total audiobooks 2 Children's 3 weeks No Limit 2 Books Type Length Limit Renewals? Adult books 3 weeks No Limit 2 Adult new books 2 weeks No Limit 2 Children's books 3 weeks 3 per series or subject 5 per holiday No other limit 2 Books (Summer Reading) 3 weeks 2 YA + 5 children's 2 Express Books 2 weeks 5 None. Music Type Length Limit Renewals? Adult CDs 3 weeks No Limit 2 Adult New CDs 2 weeks 10 total CDs 2 Music (LPs) 2 weeks 3 total LPs 2 Children's CDs 3 weeks No Limit 2 DVDs Type Length Limit Renewals? DVD & Blu-Ray 1 week 10 adult + 10 children's 2 DVD & Blu-ray (New) 1 week 5 adult + 5 children's 2 TV Series/Nonfic DVD & Blu-Ray 3 weeks 10 adult + 10 children's 2 Other Type Length Limit Renewals? Bookmobile Items 6 weeks No limit 1 Magazines 2 weeks No limit 2 Video Games 1 week 3 per person 2 Library of Things Type Length Limit Renewals? Adult/Children's/YA 2 weeks 3 items 2 Renewing Materials You may renew an item twice if it has not been requested by another patron: by phone (Main Library 508-532-5570 x4351; McAuliffe Branch 508-532-5636), through your online account , or in person. No renewals: Express Books, DVDs, new CDs, video games, all devices. Late Charges *We are currently not charging late fees for Framingham Public Library items. Replacement charges may still apply for lost or damaged items.* Lost Material If an item you borrow is lost or damaged, the current replacement cost is charged. Reserves You are welcome to reserve library materials in person, by phone, or by logging on to your account. When the items you request come in, we will notify you by mail or by email; sign up online through the online catalog for email notification. Returning Materials Library materials may be returned to either Framingham Public Library branch or any Minuteman Network library. Exceptions : Items borrowed via Interlibrary Loan, museum passes, and items that belong to the Library of Things must be returned to the library where they were picked up. Receiving Notifications If you supplied your email address when you signed up for your card, you will receive the following courtesy reminders via email: Two days before an item is due Two weeks after the due date of an item One month after the due date of an item When a requested item is available for pickup If you would like to receive courtesy reminders sent to you via text, sign up for text notification HERE . Framingham Public Library Main Library 49 Lexington Street Framingham, MA 01702 508-532-5570 508-872-2775 --> McAuliffe Branch 746 Water Street Framingham, MA 01701 508-532-5636 FPL Foundation FPL Friends Framingham Reads Together --> Connect with Us Sign up for email updates of Library news and events! pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits Up to 4 persons at half price; infants under 12 months admitted free but still need a ticket.
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "14 Vernon Street, Framingham, MA 01701","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-the-danforth_104_md.jpg","telephone": "508-215-5110","name": "The Danforth","url": "","description": "The Danforth is an art museum and school that opened in Framingham in 1975. After a closure of three years, the museum reopened in 2019 in a newly renovated location on Centre Common. It has six galleries, one dedicated to its collection of American art from the 1800s up to the present; a second containing the work of the African-American female sculptor Meta Vaux Warrick Fuller; and four used as spaces for changing exhibitions. The museum&apos;s permanent collection includes work by Gilbert Stuart, Charles Sprague Pearce, Eastman Johnson, Albert Bierstadt, and Thomas Hart Benton, as well as work by the Boston Expressionists and contemporary artists such as Faith Ringgold, Richard Yarde, Barbara Grad, Andrew Stevovich, and Jason Berger."} The Danforth 14 Vernon Street, Framingham, MA 01701508-215-5110https://danforth.framingham.edu/[Details/Reserve]
The Danforth The Danforth is an art museum and school that opened in Framingham in 1975. After a closure of three years, the museum reopened in 2019 in a newly renovated location on Centre Common. It has six galleries, one dedicated to its collection of American art from the 1800s up to the present; a second containing the work of the African-American female sculptor Meta Vaux Warrick Fuller; and four used as spaces for changing exhibitions. The museum's permanent collection includes work by Gilbert Stuart, Charles Sprague Pearce, Eastman Johnson, Albert Bierstadt, and Thomas Hart Benton, as well as work by the Boston Expressionists and contemporary artists such as Faith Ringgold, Richard Yarde, Barbara Grad, Andrew Stevovich, and Jason Berger.
Categories: Art, Children, Family
Pass Type: Circulating Pass (must be picked up and returned to the branch)
Pass Benefits Admits up to 4 persons free of charge
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "145 Redstone Hill, Sterling, MA 01564","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-davis-farmland_47_md.jpg","telephone": "978-422-6666","name": "Davis Farmland","url": "","description": "Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a &ldquo;Mega Maze&rdquo; in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises."} Davis Farmland 145 Redstone Hill, Sterling, MA 01564978-422-6666https://www.davisfarmland.com/[Details/Reserve]
Davis Farmland Davis Farmland is a seven-generation, working family farm, continuously in operation since 1846 in Sterling, MA. Starting in 1996, Davis Farmland has provided opportunities for discovery and play to families with children 12 years old and younger. It has a Waterspray Park; Imagine-Acres; Adventureland; interactions with farm animals; and a “Mega Maze” in the fall. It is also home to the largest sanctuary of endangered farm animals in North America. Special events include hands-on reptile shows. Hours change with the season. Shop with food and souvenirs on the premises.
Categories: Children, Family, Nature, Zoo
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits Pass admits up to 6 guests to Davis Farmland or Davis Mega Maze with $10 off the at-the-gate admission rate.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-discovery-museums_4_md.jpg","telephone": "978-264-4200","name": "Discovery Museum","url": "","description": "Acton&apos;s Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum&apos;s building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles. "} Discovery Museum 177 Main Street, Acton, MA 01720978-264-4200http://www.discoveryacton.org/[Details/Reserve]
Discovery Museum Acton's Discovery Museum, located on a 4-acre campus, is aimed at providing an environment in which play becomes a conduit for learning about science, technology, and nature. The museum's building contains a number of galleries dedicated to teaching scientific concepts in a fun, hands-on environment, as well as themed play spaces for younger visitors. Outdoors, Discovery Woods includes a nature playscape with a cargo net climb, nest swing and beaver lodges, as well as a treehouse with an observation deck and a bouncy bridge. There are opportunities to practice orienteering with provided maps and instructions on trails on the property. A museum shop is on the premises. The museum building is fully ADA compliant, with exhibits based on Universal Design principles.
Categories: Children, Family, Science
Pass Benefits Up to three half price admissions.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "180 Hemenway Road, Framingham, MA 01701","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-native-plant-trust_12_md.jpg","telephone": "508-877-7630","name": "Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust)","url": "","description": "The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public."} Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) 180 Hemenway Road, Framingham, MA 01701508-877-7630http://www.nativeplanttrust.org/[Details/Reserve]
Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) The Garden in the Woods (Native Plant Trust) displays rare and common native New England plants in a naturalistic setting at its location in Framingham, Massachusetts, attracting visitors from all over the world. Set on 45 acres, it includes two miles of paths that meander over glacier-sculpted ridges and through narrow valleys. A pond, a wooded bog, several springs, and a brook bring a diversity of wildlife into the garden. The garden is maintained by the Native Plant Trust, which works throughout New England to monitor and protect rare and endangered plants, collect and bank seeds for biological diversity, control invasive species, conduct research, and educate the public.
Categories: Family, Nature
Pass Benefits Admits 2 persons; adults at 1/2 price, children (5-17) $1.00
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "25 Evans Way, Boston, MA 02115","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-gardner-museum_14_md.jpg","telephone": "617-566-1401","name": "Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum","url": "","description": "The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series. "} Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum 25 Evans Way, Boston, MA 02115617-566-1401http://www.gardnermuseum.org/home/[Details/Reserve]
Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
Categories: Art, Family, Music
Pass Benefits Pass admits 4 people at $5 a person.
